content: it feels kind of pompous to pin something i wrote but I guess it does kind of define a process flow
alright I put together <#1409244176851144854> but I gotta get back to work now, I'll chat more tonight~
+1 vote against entourage, i'm French so I find it cringe as fuck
-# not to mention the hint that the MC is *not just fighting* with this lineup
I'm very interested in your perspective, could you please elaborate?

For the French part : I die inside a little bit every time I heard French words used for style points.
The word "entourage" describes the people that around "around" you, meaning your friends, colleagues, or your designated bootlickers if you're someone with power, which doesn't really align with describing a battle team.

Now for the english side of things, the only time I've seen it used was harem settings (*maybe I saw it once for something happening in a royal court*), but still with the original French meaning … you can guess what that implies for the relation between the keeper & the team.
-# hint : they're not just around the keeper, they're on top, below, everywhere …

It might just be me, but reading it here I can't really not think of that.

ahh. it's interesting you say that about the english usage, because as a semantics guy I recognize but tend to dismiss the significance *(up to a certain point)* of the usage of a word for a purpose that has its own dedicated (or more connotative) word, in this case Escort and Consort.

As for it being a French word, let's be real: at least in American English, foreign words are always at least a little cooler than domestic words. I'm also of the opinion that since I'm participating in *English* localization, I'm less concerned about what speakers of another language "see" in the usage of their loan words, but I am 1000% open to being completely wrong and up my own ass about that. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202> 
-# also I haven't started any frenzy stages yet but I'm pretty sure what I know about them can corroborate that connotation lol
which is also why i *really* would like it if whoever they hire for the localization team is added to this discord so we have an authority to ask in case of questions

Also I would like to note this response from Ace regarding the work I'm doing and its scope, for whatever it's worth.
yeah that response has also been in part as to why i've been hesitant on making any changes too large

"10% bonus credits for pre-paid deposits!"

oh
marketing speak in china likes to use numbers instead of percentages
yeah that makes more sense
marketing stuff in the west tends to prefer percentages more, at least that's how I feel
here you see a lot of 20% off, 30% off, etc
that was definitely a cultural difference
because I would never have understood that
*I wrote preorder on my draft btw LUL*
yeah this is like, exactly the kind of thing that it'd be best to have a proper professional localization expert around for
editing MTL is such a challenge just because so many lines out of context make zero sense and you're kind of left going ???

referring to the next chunk of her response, for sure. this is why I've been focused less right now on *actually rewriting lines of text* and more on the bigger picture. 
The information I'm gathering is important both for getting the stuff that needs to be done done, and for creating a guide comprehensive enough for it to be translated back into Chinese so that the devs can utilize it.

Also I have the "obsessive" kind of OCD so tweaking just "a couple things" when I see *almost everything* as wrong in some way is a lil tough but that's a me problem.

besides, someone else was already working on the "this is entirely broken" stuff, so I figured they can work on that in the short term.

also this doesn't *really* matter since the whole context of the convo is daffodil sending you spam marketing ads that are total scams so the actual specifics of the promo don't matter, but 
> 交换商店酬宾活动，神秘福袋礼品抽取，三头一次，十头三次，预存十头送一头，欢迎光临选购。
the MTL isn't really clear on this i think, but technically the translation should be something like
> Exchange Shop Promotion: Mystery Gift Draw! Buy three, roll once! Buy ten, roll three times! If you pre-pay for ten, we'll throw in one for free! Welcome!
like, the mystery draw is separate from buying whatever she's actually selling
so it's even more of a scam because you're not actually getting more of what she's selling
you're just getting these mystery draws that are probably just garbage

also the unit used for whatever she's selling is 头, or head, which can be a counter word but also might just be literal "head" since daffodil buys heads (see: ch4 she buys francis's head as payment for giving francis the anti-hounds of tindalos rune knowledge)
so basically the whole thing is just this super vague marketing ad that's 100% a scam
also did anyone notice that she says buy 3 get 1 roll and buy 10 get 3 rolls but that's actually a worse deal
because technically you could just buy 9 and get 3 rolls
this shit has *layers* man omg daffodil

I would like to reiterate that this is such a fantastic writeup that I think you should pin it if you have the permission, Lens! It's an excellent thing to reference for anyone new to this kind of project and it'd be great to just tell folks to <:pins:895019904024068167> to find it~
I like Entourage for flavor
i prefer team/party since I think basic gaming terminology should be consistent and simple to understand
fancier terms should be used for unique mechanics but the idea of a party is fairly standard across games
lineup is fine because it's a separate concept from that of the team itself
team is the team, lineup is the composition of the team

I refute your point by agreeing with it. The fact that the idea of a team or party is so blatantly standard across all games means that we have some space to make the game feel *less basic.* 
And if we make it clear at all points, in all menus, that the word we're using is Entourage, and make new players "assemble their entourage" during the tutorial, then players will understand what it means when a Relic gives you "`Entourage Crit Rate +50%`."

joining in.. 
I would give 1 vote for entourage. the reason?, first, it gives off different vibes compared to another similar game. second, it vibes with the style of the games lore in the way that word refers to .. well.. group of special people (according to my dictionary).. so, +1 from me for Entourage
actually I have a special argument
they renamed DPS/Tank/Support
also, to mention the lineup, it was usually either called a lineup or roster
so unless that was in error

yeah it's Assault/Warden/Chorus

sorry i mistook your intentions
so I am 100% down for this unless Ace says otherwise

Yes, I agree with this.. because they use different terms, it gives off diffrent vibes too
even though the current one is a mistake, *lets ask for that to be an official, shall we?*
I lean conservative in translation philosophy so I remain against it

though, one small problem, that I'll need help brainstorming for.
the Wheels of Destiny that describe their effects as `Team Unique:`. I already didn't like the way this is worded but I damn well can't think of anything better even if we *didn't* change to Entourage.
`Entourage Unique:` sounds baaaaaaaaaad.
well, what's the original CN term?
I think it's important to pay attention to what changes need to be made for the sake of clarity, and what changes you want to make *for the sake of making changes*

1) could just be unique
2) or it could just be renamed to "Unique" since WoDs are already unique inherently
the only thing I can think of would be like the hand limit
but even that's worded specifically IIRC
the question here to me is, at what point has there been any confusion or inaccuracy from anyone due to the term Team being used
and if there is none, why does a change need to be made and what purpose would it serve
well the argument isn't about confusion or accuracy
Lineup/Team/Party/You/"All Awakeners" are all used interchangeably
please keep in mind that we are here to communicate the dev's intentions into a different language as accurately as possible
our job is not to assign intention or to create meaning where there is none
the devs have a vision for their game, and our role as translators is to convey the voice of the original work as accurately as possible
that said I really do wish we had a direct link to either a dev or a narrative designer because it's certainly one of those things where I could see them wanting it
but without active confirmation I always lean conservative in terms of changes made
队伍 in all cases as far as I can tell, which literally just means team
i suppose party or troop could work
then in this case, keep both options available, see which one favors more after the case is brought to the higher ups
"entourage" to me has more specific implications in terms of followers rather than comrades

You are, by all means and measures, absolutely correct.
My argument is, however, that if the original language of a work does not have a word available to them that conveys the mood of the work as well as what another language may have, is it better to keep the less-interesting word for the sake of transliteration?
also as another aside I'm also just loathe to make any large-scale sweeping changes in terms of terminology unless actively necessary, solely due to the amount of work it'd take
because changing team, for example, means we're probably going to need to make changes to like literally thousands of lines across the entire game which is...a lot of work for very little gain

(this is the point, yes)
Entourage describes a group of people that come together *specifically because of* one uniting individual.
A General has a team.
A President has an entourage.

Work I'm already doing.
Literally *thousands* of lines, to make terminology consistent throughout the game.
I've already committed myself to this task and I intend to see it through.
I may as well make all of these decisions before I get too far in so that I don't have to go back through it.
the problem to me is that an entourage implies a leading individual
or at least some individual of mugh higher importance

the keeper is the primary figure about as much as a power bank is the primary tool for your electronics on a camping trip
the characters that move the story along are by and large the awakeners, not the keeper

Agreed, but that's not a refutation of the word choice of Entourage.
the idea of an entourage is also antithesis to equality and the climactic scenes of ch9 are literally all about comradery and partnership
"the hero and his entourage fight the demon king" is somewhat less attractive of a concept than "the hero and his comrades fight the demon king"
like I suppose it wouldn't necessarily be *wrong*, but it's certainly somewhat dismissive to his party members

okay, cool, sure, but that example contains the glaring error that you're trying to pick a word to describe a horizontal power structure but starting the sentence with "the hero and".
at no point do I endorse referring to "The Keeper and their entourage", and the only scenario in which I would see that distinction occurring in the text would be the tutorial instructing you to build your party, in which we would use "assemble *an* entourage".
just add it to the "ask devs" list
no harm in asking and it'd help if we could get clarification on how literal they want us to be anyways
I don't believe Entourage has as much of that connotation as you do, because we have plenty of words that are used to communicate that connotation, most specifically **Retinue**.

the real question at hand here, because it was never made terribly clear (at least to me), is whether our job is Transliteration, Translation, or Localization.
Seeing as the game has already been fully transliterated into English (by AI), I was under the assumption that our job was localization.
weeeelll, technically our job is to proofread
also localization is a spectrum and what level our work should fall under in that spectrum is up to the original author always
which again would be nice to have some dev insight on but in the absense of it it's safer to be conservative than to make changes which need to either be rolled back or veto'd
obligatory

personally, I see the discussion of picking between Team / Party / Lineup and picking Entourage after much deliberation and the discussion of picking between Kum / Koom / Kumu and picking Koum after much deliberation to be basically the same.

with that said, I submit that I am speaking from absolutely zero experience in translating, and having said my whole peace, I will proceed to close my mouth and open my ears.
yep that is correct, but that's also why I follow a process for koum

Primarily:
1. First, deciding on the necessity of the change - could things be left as is, and if not, why do they need to be changed
1a. Determining the impact of leaving things as is - a search on the discord finds quite literally *hundreds* of messages of people either complaining or making fun of her name specifically, meaning it's a very visual issue that is clearly having a very noticeable impact
1b. Thus, the answer I arrived at was yes, things do need to be changed - the reason is that the current spellings are inconsistent and cause confusion, and must be standardized

2. Next, attempting to trace the original authorial intent given the lack of developer insight - that's why I spent so much time trying to figure out the source of the name

3. Next, verifying how best to proceed with the change - as the purpose of the change is primarily to ensure consistency across all instances of the name, the most important aspect is ensuring that consistency and the secondary aspects are making sure that it can be rolled back and making sure that it can be changed to something else if desired

4. Next, since the two options I arrived at were qom and koum, I checked the sheet for all instances of either string - qom returned matches on the key they use for field detection, while koum returned zero matches. Based on this, I concluded that using the string "koum" would provide a unique identifier that can be easily replaced in all instances it shows up if at any point it is veto'd or changed (which was the main reason I went with it)

5. Next, following established process of updating the terms sheet in the suggested column and providing my notes and logic for arriving at that term

at this point I have created a pull request but I have also split the main impact of Koum naming (story ch4) and any other instances of name changes (flavor text, items, character details, etc) into separate files so they can be separated and removed if desired

in essence, it's a non-trivial decision made after a considerable amount of thought which was only sparked out of necessity and done with as much direct understanding of authorial intent as is current available to us
with specifically designed ways to either remove the change or make future changes if desired
I don't *like* doing it because again, I lean conservative towards making any non-necessary changes, but in this case it did feel necessary
You should pin this! Thank you for taking the time to educate
again, I think there's no issue or harm with asking Ace to ask the devs right?
besides it'd help with clarifying exactly what we're doing
ofc, it's been noted here that all of this discussion is due to *the lack of developer insight*
there isn't, but the list of asks grows by the day and I think we're more active than ace can keep up with so I don't think we're going to get an answer for everything, unfortunately
I'm already busy writing up a fully contextualized post to put in <#1404748432328888401>
in the current void of answers the best we can do is do what we can with what we've got, and make sure to leave an option for reversion at any point

esp for me since I have times in texts where characters say something that either makes no sense (Daffodil's text about her sale), say things that were TL'd extremely literally (leaving me to have to figure out how best to write the sentence while preserving the meaning), or just talk weirdly in general
the project as a whole will also be a very iterative thing as well, there's no chance we can big bang everything into a fully perfected form in one pass

yeah I think the original intent was just "does this sound like a good idea" and I think the idea works
doesn't mean we have to commit to it yet
so like I said, we can always ask the devs what they think
it's 100% just a matter of "what is the scope of what we're doing"
feel free to ask me for anything that doesn't make sense, I feel like that's going to come up quite a lot and is the main challenge with working on editing only with no understanding of the original
fortunately I do have enough understanding of chinese to figure out original meanings/intents so I've been able to handle instances of that by myself, but other people won't
so the only thing you can do is ask someone who can

I'd like to discuss the inconsistency of the terms used to describe the menu in which you select the 4 Awakeners that will accompany you into an exploration, and the terms used to describe said conglomerate of Awakeners.
The menu is called **Lineup**. Wheels and Covenants describe some effects as **Team** Unique. The button to quick-select Awakeners says Quick **Team Lineup**. The default name for each of the 10 setups you can have is **Party**(N). The menu to see collective stats is called **Team** Information. Once in an exploration, any collectively applied buffs are described using a random mix of **Team**, **You**, **All Awakeners**, or simply with no pronoun whatsoever. "`Crit Rate +50%`".

If it were up to me, I'd go the "let's get fancy" route and standardize all of these descriptions to **Entourage**. Why? First, it sounds cool as hell. Style points, yo. Second, it does a great job of reflecting the diagetic situation, being that the Awakeners have more of a compositional connection to the Keeper than to the other members of their "team".

If **Entourage** is too much of a mouthful though, my second pick was **Troupe**, but at that point I'd rather just default back to **Team**.
